UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the OmniVid AI application, storing their essential profile information linked to their Google account. No sensitive authentication data like passwords are stored here.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. (Primary Key)

googleId.description: The unique identifier provided by Google OAuth for the user, used to link the user's Google account to their profile in the app.

email.description: The user's email address obtained from Google, used for identification and communication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created in the system.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user successfully logged into the application.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user requested account deletion. This field is used for soft deletion to comply with GDPR/CCPA, allowing for potential recovery within a grace period if necessary. Null if the account is active.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a single AI-generated video, including its generation parameters, current status, and storage location. This entity tracks the lifecycle and details of each video produced by the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity. (Primary Key)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who initiated the video generation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Video)

originalPrompt.description: The raw text prompt initially provided by the user for video generation, in their native language.

optimizedPrompt.description: The AI-enhanced and translated (typically to English) prompt used internally by the video generation engine for better results.

inputLanguage.description: The ISO 639-1 language code (or similar identifier) of the original prompt provided by the user.

outputLanguage.description: The ISO 639-1 language code (or similar identifier) chosen for the video's voiceover or subtitles.

visualStyle.description: The chosen or AI-suggested visual style applied to the video (e.g., 'Cinematic', 'Anime', 'Realistic').

status.description: The current status of the video generation process (e.g., 'queued', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed', 'blocked').

generationMode.description: Indicates the generation queue used, either 'Standard' (unlimited, longer queue) or 'FastPass' (ad-supported, accelerated generation).

resolution.description: The resolution of the generated video (e.g., '720p').

storageUrl.description: The secure URL to the generated video file stored in Google Cloud Storage.

thumbnailUrl.description: The secure URL to a thumbnail image of the video, used for previewing.

durationSeconds.description: The total duration of the generated video in seconds.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the video generation request was initiated by the user.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the video generation process was successfully completed and the video became available. Null if not yet completed.

isWatermarked.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the video contains an invisible digital watermark (always true for the free tier to identify it as AI-generated).

contentBlockedReason.description: If the video generation was blocked due to content safety violations, this field provides the reason (e.g., 'violence', 'illegal content', 'deepfake'). Null if not blocked.

definition.description: Stores a user's profile information. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching `userId`. The document's `id` field must match the `userId` in the path for robust ownership verification, supporting authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual video details generated by a user. The `userId` from the path is explicitly stored in each `Video` document (`resource.data.userId`) for robust ownership verification, ensuring authorization independence. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching `userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this video, matching `request.auth.uid` and `resource.data.userId`.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific video.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for OmniVid AI is designed around a hierarchical, user-centric model, prioritizing strong authorization independence, simple security rules, and efficient querying capabilities.

1.  **User-Centric Root Collections:** The primary collection is `/users/{userId}`, directly representing individual `UserProfile` entities. This path-based ownership is fundamental for securing private user data. The `userId` in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, making authorization checks straightforward and atomic for user profiles.

2.  **Hierarchical Subcollections for User Data:** User-generated videos are stored in a subcollection: `/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`. This continues the hierarchical ownership model, clearly indicating that `Video` entities belong to a specific `UserProfile`.

3.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL - Denormalization Strategy):**
    *   For `UserProfile` documents at `/users/{userId}`, the `id` field within the `UserProfile` document is explicitly designed to match the `{userId}` in the path (`resource.data.id == userId`). This explicit mapping of the `userId` within the document (which is implicitly the owner) ensures that rules do not need to perform `get()` operations on parent documents to determine ownership. Authorization is solely based on `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, and optionally validating `resource.data.id` against the path `userId` for data integrity.
    *   For `Video` documents at `/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`, the `Video` entity already includes a `userId` field. This `userId` field *must* match the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This explicit denormalization of the `ownerId` (`userId`) within each `Video` document allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == userId` (from path) AND `resource.data.userId == userId` (from document). This eliminates any need to `get()` the parent `UserProfile` document to determine who owns the video. Consequently, actions like creating a `Video` can be atomic without requiring a separate `UserProfile` lookup in rules, enhancing performance and simplifying rule logic.

4.  **Support for Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs - Rules are not Filters):**
    *   **Structural Segregation:** All documents within `/users/{userId}` (UserProfile) share the same security posture (owner-only). Similarly, all documents within `/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}` (Video) also share the same security posture (owner-only). There's no mixing of public/private data within the same collection, which would otherwise require complex filtering in rules that cannot be enforced for `list` operations.
    *   **Efficient Listing:** A user can `list` all their own videos by querying the `/users/{request.auth.uid}/videos` subcollection. The security rules can easily enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the path for *all* reads (get/list), ensuring that users can only retrieve their own content. The denormalized `userId` field in the `Video` documents further strengthens this by making the ownership explicit within each document, allowing for a robust check that `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` without requiring complex query filters or `get()` calls. This structure inherently supports QAPs by ensuring that any `list` operation is already constrained by the authenticated user's ID, making the server-side security rule simple and efficient.

This structure inherently scales well, as user data is sharded by `userId`, preventing hot spots and allowing for efficient, owner-scoped queries.
